subject: Project Management
title: Breaking It Down
description: A micro-lesson that makes Breaking It Down usable.

hook.text: Estimating a whole project is hard. Estimating each tiny task is much easier — and more accurate.

buildup.text: A Work Breakdown Structure splits a project into hundreds of small, estimable pieces.

discovery.text: Small tasks are less likely to be underestimated because we can picture exactly what's involved.

twist.text: But decomposition has a trap: it misses integration work — the effort of gluing all the pieces together.

climax.text: Break it down, then add a buffer for what the breakdown missed. That's the practical sweet spot.

punchline.text: Small estimates sum to better plans.

quiz.question: What is a risk of breaking projects into small tasks for estimation?
options: It can miss the integration effort of combining all the pieces
Small tasks are impossible to estimate
